title: Executive Power of State
explanation: Vests the executive power of the state in the Governor, to be exercised either directly or through officers subordinate to him in accordance with the Constitution.
importantPoints: Governor is the head of the state executive
Power exercised directly or via subordinate ministers
Does not prevent Parliament/Legislature from conferring functions on subordinate authorities

title: Appointment of Governor
explanation: Stipulates that the Governor of a state shall be appointed by the President of India by warrant under his hand and seal, following a system modeled on the Canadian pattern.
importantPoints: Appointed by the President, not directly elected
Acts as a vital link between the Union and the State
Helps maintain federal equilibrium
memoryTrick: 155 = President signs and sends the Governor alive.

title: Term of Office of Governor
explanation: States that the Governor holds office during the pleasure of the President for a normal term of 5 years, though he may resign or be removed earlier.
importantPoints: Normal term is 5 years
Holds office during the absolute pleasure of the President
Can submit resignation to the President at any time
memoryTrick: 156 = 5 + 6 years? No, exactly 5 years under Presidential pleasure.

title: Qualifications for Appointment as Governor
explanation: Lays down the basic qualifications required for appointment as a Governor: must be a citizen of India and have completed the age of 35 years.
importantPoints: Must be a citizen of India
Minimum age requirement is 35 years
Conventionally an outsider to the state

title: Conditions of Governor's Office
explanation: Specifies that the Governor cannot be a member of either house of Parliament or State Legislature, cannot hold any other office of profit, and enjoys official residence and emoluments determined by Parliament.
importantPoints: Cannot be an MP or MLA concurrently
Entitled to rent-free official residence (Raj Bhavan)
Emoluments cannot be diminished during term

title: Oath or Affirmation by the Governor
explanation: Mandates that the Governor, before entering office, must make and subscribe an oath in the presence of the Chief Justice of the State High Court (or senior-most judge available).
importantPoints: Oath administered by the Chief Justice of the State High Court
Commits to preserving, protecting, and defending the Constitution
Dedicated to service of the people
memoryTrick: 159 = High Court Chief Justice administers the oath.

title: Discharge of Governor's Functions in Contingencies
explanation: Empowers the President to make provisions for discharging the functions of the Governor in any contingency not provided for in Chapter II (e.g., sudden illness or vacancy).
importantPoints: President handles unexpected gubernatorial vacancies
Ensures continuity of state administration
Usually assigns duty to the state High Court Chief Justice

title: Power of Governor to Grant Pardons, etc.
explanation: Grants the Governor the power to grant pardons, reprieves, respites, or remissions of punishment in respect of state law violations, excluding death sentences and court-martials.
importantPoints: Covers matters extending to state executive power
Cannot pardon a death sentence (Presidential exclusive)
Cannot pardon court-martial punishments
memoryTrick: 161 = State pardoning floor (no death penalty access).

title: Extent of Executive Power of State
explanation: Defines the territorial and subject-matter scope of the state's executive power, extending to matters with respect to which the State Legislature has power to make laws.
importantPoints: Covers State List subjects
Subject to proviso regarding concurrent list matters
Aligns executive authority with legislative competence

title: Council of Ministers to Aid and Advise Governor
explanation: Establishes that there shall be a Council of Ministers with the Chief Minister at the head to aid and advise the Governor, except in matters where he is required to act in his discretion.
importantPoints: Ministerial advice is normally binding
Governor's discretionary actions cannot be questioned in court
Core pillar of parliamentary democracy in states

title: Other Provisions as to Ministers
explanation: Deals with the appointment of the Chief Minister by the Governor and other ministers on CM's advice, collective responsibility, and oath of office.
importantPoints: CM appointed by Governor; ministers appointed on CM's advice
Collectively responsible to the Legislative Assembly
Size capped at 15 percent of total Assembly strength (91st Amendment)
memoryTrick: 164 = Chief Minister appointment and cabinet cap.

title: Advocate-General for the State
explanation: Provides for the appointment of the highest law officer of the state by the Governor, who must be qualified to be a judge of a High Court.
importantPoints: Highest legal advisor to the state government
Has right to speak in proceedings of state legislature without voting rights
Holds office during the pleasure of the Governor

title: Conduct of Business of the Government of a State
explanation: Requires all executive actions of the government of a state to be expressed to be taken in the name of the Governor and regulates the allocation of business among ministers.
importantPoints: All state orders issued in Governor's name
Rules made by Governor for convenient transaction of government business
Orders authenticated as prescribed by state rules

title: Duties of Chief Minister as Respects Furnishing Information to Governor
explanation: Outlines the constitutional duty of the Chief Minister to communicate to the Governor all decisions of the Council of Ministers and furnish information relating to state administration.
importantPoints: CM acts as the formal communication channel to the Governor
Governor can call for specific administrative details
Ensures transparency between executive branches
memoryTrick: 167 = Chief Minister's report card to Governor.

title: Constitution of Legislatures in States
explanation: States that for every state there shall be a Legislature which consists of the Governor and two Houses (Legislative Council and Legislative Assembly) or one House (Assembly).
importantPoints: Establishes bicameral or unicameral state legislatures
Governor is an integral component of the legislature
Most states have unicameral assemblies

title: Abolition or Creation of Legislative Councils in States
explanation: Empowers Parliament to create or abolish a State Legislative Council if the Legislative Assembly passes a resolution to that effect by a special majority.
importantPoints: Initiated by state assembly special majority
Enacted by simple majority ordinary legislation in Parliament
Not classified as a constitutional amendment under Article 368
memoryTrick: 169 = Parliament creates or deletes upper houses.

title: Composition of the Legislative Assemblies
explanation: Specifies that the Legislative Assembly of each state shall consist of not more than 500 and not less than 60 members chosen by direct election from territorial constituencies.
importantPoints: Directly elected by adult suffrage
Upper limit 500, lower limit 60 (with historical exceptions)
Constituencies readjusted after each census

title: Composition of the Legislative Councils
explanation: Sets the composition rules for the upper house (Vidhan Parishad): total membership cannot exceed one-third of the Assembly, with a minimum of 40 members, drawn from diverse electorates.
importantPoints: Max 1/3rd of Assembly size, minimum 40
Electorates include graduates, teachers, local bodies, MLAs, and gubernatorial nominees
Permanent, non-dissolvable house

title: Duration of State Legislatures
explanation: Fixes the normal duration of the Legislative Assembly at 5 years from its first meeting unless dissolved earlier, while establishing that the Legislative Council is not subject to dissolution.
importantPoints: Assembly term is 5 years unless dissolved prematurely
Legislative Council is a permanent body (1/3rd retire every 2 years)
Extension possible during National Emergency

title: Qualification for Membership of the State Legislature
explanation: Lays down qualifications: citizen of India, minimum age of 25 years for Assembly and 30 years for Council, and possession of other qualifications prescribed by Parliament.
importantPoints: Minimum age: 25 (Assembly), 30 (Council)
Must take oath before Election Commission representative
Must be a registered voter in the state

title: Sessions of the State Legislature, Prorogation and Dissolution
explanation: Empowers the Governor to summon each house, prorogue them, and dissolve the Legislative Assembly.
importantPoints: Maximum gap between two sessions cannot exceed 6 months
Governor dissolves Assembly on Chief Minister's advice
Prorogation terminates a session, not the house

title: Right of Governor to Address and Send Messages to Houses
explanation: Empowers the Governor to address either or both houses assembled together and send messages regarding pending bills or other matters.
importantPoints: Can address legislature at any time
Legislature must consider messages sent without delay
Enhances legislative executive communication

title: Special Address by the Governor
explanation: Mandates the Governor to address the state legislature at the commencement of the first session after each general election and the first session of each year.
importantPoints: Special annual and post-election address
Outlines government policies and legislative agenda
Requires discussion on the address (motion of thanks)

title: Rights of Ministers and Advocate-General as Respects Houses
explanation: Grants every minister and the Advocate-General the right to speak in and take part in the proceedings of either house, though they cannot vote if not members.
importantPoints: Right of audience in legislative houses
Advocate-General can speak but not vote
Ministers can participate in houses they do not belong to
memoryTrick: 177 = Ministers and AG free speech pass.

title: The Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Legislative Assembly
explanation: Requires every State Legislative Assembly to choose two members to be respectively Speaker and Deputy Speaker.
importantPoints: Ensures internal democratic leadership of Assembly
Presides over Assembly sittings
Maintains order and decorum

title: Vacation and Resignation of, and Removal from, Speaker and Deputy Speaker
explanation: Sets rules: vacation of office upon ceasing to be a member, resignation in writing to each other, or removal by a resolution passed by a majority of all then members of the Assembly.
importantPoints: Requires 14 days' advance notice for removal resolution
Must vacate office if membership terminates
Resignations exchanged mutually

title: Power of Deputy Speaker or Other Person to Perform Duties of Speaker
explanation: Outlines that during the absence of the Speaker, the Deputy Speaker shall perform duties, and if both are absent, such member as may be determined by rules of procedure.
importantPoints: Uninterrupted house functioning during vacancies
Deputy Speaker acts as Speaker
Panel of chairpersons steps in if necessary

title: The Speaker or Deputy Speaker Not to Preside While a Resolution for Removal is Under Consideration
explanation: Prohibits the Speaker or Deputy Speaker from presiding over a sitting while a resolution for their removal is under consideration, though they retain the right to speak and vote.
importantPoints: Prevents conflict of interest during removal motions
Can participate in debate and initial voting
Presiding chair taken by another member
memoryTrick: 181 = Step down from chair when facing removal.

title: The Chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Legislative Council
explanation: Mandates that every State Legislative Council shall choose two members to be Chairman and Deputy Chairman.
importantPoints: Upper house leadership officers
Elected from among Council members
Equivalent to Rajya Sabha Chairman/Deputy Chairman roles

title: Vacation and Resignation of, and Removal from, Chairman and Deputy Chairman
explanation: Governs resignation, vacation, and removal procedures for the Chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Legislative Council.
importantPoints: Mirrors Assembly speaker removal rules
Requires majority resolution
14 days notice period

title: Power of Deputy Chairman or Other Person to Perform Duties of Chairman
explanation: Empowers the Deputy Chairman to perform duties during the absence of the Chairman.
importantPoints: Ensures continuity in upper house chair
Similar mechanism to Article 180

title: The Chairman or Deputy Chairman Not to Preside While Resolution for Removal is Under Consideration
explanation: Restricts the Chairman or Deputy Chairman from presiding while their removal resolution is active.
importantPoints: Impartiality safeguard
Mirrors Article 181
memoryTrick: 185 = Council chair steps down during trial.

title: Salaries and Allowances of the Speaker and Deputy Speaker and Chairman and Deputy Chairman
explanation: Provides that salaries and allowances of presiding officers shall be fixed by the state legislature and charged upon the Consolidated Fund of the State.
importantPoints: Charged expenditure (non-votable)
Guarantees financial independence of presiding officers
memoryTrick: 186 = Presiding officers' pay charged on state fund.

title: Secretariat of State Legislature
explanation: Provides for a separate secretarial staff for each house of the state legislature.
importantPoints: Independent administrative staff for legislature
Prevents executive interference in legislative secretariats

title: Oath or Affirmation by Members
explanation: Requires every member of the Legislative Assembly or Council to take an oath or affirmation before the Governor or a person appointed by him.
importantPoints: Mandatory before taking seat in house
Pledge of allegiance to Constitution

title: Voting in Houses, Power of Houses to Act Notwithstanding Vacancies and Quorum
explanation: Sets quorum at 10 members or one-tenth of total house membership (whichever is greater), with voting by majority and casting vote by presiding officer in ties.
importantPoints: Quorum rule: 1/10th of house or 10 members
Presiding officer exercises casting vote only in ties
Proceedings valid despite casual vacancies

title: Vacation of Seats
explanation: Deals with disqualification scenarios, dual membership bans (Parliament and State Legislature simultaneously), and resignation procedures.
importantPoints: Cannot hold seats in both Parliament and State Legislature
Seat vacates if absent without permission for 60 days
Resignation accepted by Speaker/Chairman
memoryTrick: 190 = Seat vacation and dual membership ban.

title: Disqualifications for Membership
explanation: Lists grounds for disqualification: office of profit, unsound mind, undischarged insolvency, loss of citizenship, or disqualification under the Tenth Schedule (Anti-Defection Law).
importantPoints: Office of profit prohibition
Includes anti-defection disqualification (10th Schedule)
Determined by Governor in consultation with Election Commission

title: Decision on Questions as to Disqualifications of Members
explanation: Stipulates that if any question arises whether a member has become subject to disqualification, the question shall be referred to the Governor, whose decision shall be final (after obtaining Election Commission opinion).
importantPoints: Governor decides disqualification disputes
Must obtain and follow the Election Commission's opinion
Judicial review applies to arbitrary orders
memoryTrick: 192 = Governor decides disqualifications with EC advice.

title: Penalty for Sitting and Voting Before Taking Oath or When Unqualified
explanation: Imposes a penalty of 500 rupees per day for any person who sits or votes in the house knowing they are not qualified or are disqualified.
importantPoints: Financial penalty for unlawful participation
Recoverable as a debt due to the state

title: Powers, Privileges, etc., of the Houses of Legislatures and of the Members and Committees Thereof
explanation: Guarantees freedom of speech inside the legislature and protects publication of proceedings from legal action.
importantPoints: Legislative privileges similar to parliamentary privileges
Immunity from court proceedings for speeches made in house
Protects internal house autonomy

title: Salaries and Allowances of Members
explanation: Provides that members of the Legislative Assembly and Council shall be entitled to receive salaries and allowances determined by the state legislature.
importantPoints: Determined by state law, not constitutional prescription
Subject to change by ordinary legislation

title: Provisions as to Introduction and Passing of Bills
explanation: Outlines rules for ordinary bills, stating that a bill does not lapse on prorogation, though bills pending in the Assembly lapse on its dissolution.
importantPoints: Lapsing rules for ordinary bills on assembly dissolution
Prorogation does not affect pending bills
Originates in either house

title: Restriction on Powers of Legislative Council as to Bills Other Than Money Bills
explanation: Limits the power of the upper house over ordinary bills, restricting cumulative delay to a maximum of 4 months.
importantPoints: Council is a revising chamber, not a veto chamber
Assembly can override council objections
Maximum 4-month delay cap

title: Special Procedure in Respect of Money Bills
explanation: Mandates that Money Bills can only be introduced in the Legislative Assembly and restricts the Legislative Council's review window to 14 days.
importantPoints: Assembly exclusivity for Money Bills
Council cannot reject or amend, only suggest recommendations within 14 days
Requires prior recommendation of Governor

title: Definition of 'Money Bills'
explanation: Defines what constitutes a Money Bill in a state legislature (taxation, borrowing, Consolidated Fund expenditure), with the Speaker's decision being final.
importantPoints: Mirror of Article 110
Speaker's certificate that a bill is a Money Bill is final
Excludes fines and fees for local services

title: Assent to Bills
explanation: Outlines options available to the Governor when a bill is presented: grant assent, withhold assent, return ordinary bill for reconsideration, or reserve it for the President.
importantPoints: Four distinct gubernatorial assent options
Can reserve state bills for presidential scrutiny
Mandatory reservation if bill threatens High Court position

title: Bills Reserved for Consideration
explanation: Details procedure when a bill is reserved for the President under Article 200, giving the President absolute veto power even if re-passed by the state legislature.
importantPoints: President can give assent, withhold assent, or direct reconsideration
President has absolute veto on reserved state bills
State legislature is not empowered to override presidential veto
memoryTrick: 201 = President's absolute veto on state bills.

title: Annual Financial Statement (State Budget)
explanation: Requires the Governor to cause to be laid before both houses of the state legislature an annual financial statement showing estimated receipts and expenditure.
importantPoints: State budget documentation
Distinguishes between charged expenditure and votable expenditure
Mirror of Article 112

title: Procedure in Legislature With Respect to Estimates
explanation: Establishes that estimates charged on the Consolidated Fund are not submitted to vote, while other estimates are submitted in the form of demands for grants to the Assembly.
importantPoints: Charged expenditure is non-votable
Demands for grants submitted exclusively to Legislative Assembly
Council cannot vote on demands for grants

title: Appropriation Bills
explanation: Provides for the introduction of an Appropriation Bill after grants have been made by the Assembly to authorize withdrawal of money from the Consolidated Fund.
importantPoints: Authorizes legal withdrawal of state funds
No amendments can be proposed to alter grant amounts

title: Supplementary, Additional or Excess Grants
explanation: Allows presentation of supplementary or excess budget demands when sanctioned money proves insufficient.
importantPoints: Covers budget overruns and unforeseen expenses
Requires Assembly approval

title: Votes on Account, Votes of Credit and Exceptional Grants
explanation: Empowers the Assembly to make grants in advance for estimated expenditure before voting is completed (Vote on Account).
importantPoints: Interim financial authorization
Ensures government functioning during budget debate periods

title: Special Provisions as to Financial Bills
explanation: Stipulates that financial bills (other than Money Bills) cannot be introduced without the Governor's recommendation and cannot be introduced in the Legislative Council.
importantPoints: Governor's recommendation required for financial bills
Restricted introduction to Assembly

title: Rules of Procedure
explanation: Empowers each house of the state legislature to make rules for regulating its procedure and conduct of business.
importantPoints: Internal house autonomy in rule-making
Committees constituted under these rules

title: Regulation by Law of Procedure in the Legislature of the State in Relation to Financial Business
explanation: Permits the state legislature to regulate financial business through law to ensure timely completion of budgetary processes.
importantPoints: Prevents intentional financial filibustering
Streamlines state budget timelines

title: Language to be Used in the Legislature
explanation: Stipulates that business in state legislatures shall be transacted in the official language or Hindi or English, with presiding officers permitting members unable to express themselves in those languages to speak in their mother tongue.
importantPoints: Language flexibility for regional representatives
Official state language or English/Hindi standard

title: Restriction on Discussion in the Legislature
explanation: Prohibits any discussion in the state legislature with respect to the conduct of any judge of the Supreme Court or of a High Court in the discharge of judicial duties.
importantPoints: Protects judicial independence
Bars debate on individual judicial verdicts in assembly
memoryTrick: 211 = No discussing judge conduct in assembly.

title: Courts Not to Inquire into Proceedings of the Legislature
explanation: Bars courts from inquiring into the validity of any proceedings in the state legislature on the ground of any alleged irregularity of procedure.
importantPoints: Judicial non-interference in internal house procedure
Procedural autonomy of state legislatures
memoryTrick: 212 = Courts stay out of assembly proceedings.

title: Definitions (Part IX - Panchayats)
explanation: Provides definitions for terms used in Part IX relating to Panchayats, including 'district', 'Gram Sabha', 'intermediate level', 'panchayat', and 'village'.
importantPoints: Foundation definition article for Part IX
Defines village and district levels
Added by 73rd Amendment Act

title: Gram Sabha
explanation: Stipulates that a Gram Sabha may exercise such powers and perform such functions at the village level as the legislature of a state may determine.
importantPoints: Foundation of direct democracy at village level
Consists of all registered voters in a village area
Social audit and local oversight body

title: Constitution of Panchayats
explanation: Mandates the constitution in every state of Panchayats at the village, intermediate, and district levels (three-tier system), except in states with a population under 20 lakhs.
importantPoints: Three-tier system mandated across India
Exemption for smaller states under 20 lakh population from intermediate tier
Structural backbone of rural local governance

title: Composition of Panchayats
explanation: Deals with the composition of panchayats, providing that all seats shall be filled by persons chosen by direct election from territorial constituencies.
importantPoints: Direct elections for all panchayat tier seats
Chairpersons at intermediate and district levels can have indirect representation
State legislatures determine chairperson election modalities

title: Reservation of Seats
explanation: Provides for mandatory reservation of seats for SCs and STs in proportion to their population and not less than one-third reservation of total seats for women.
importantPoints: Not less than 33% reservation for women
Proportional reservation for SCs and STs
Applicable across all three tiers

title: Duration of Panchayats, etc.
explanation: Fixes a 5-year tenure for every panchayat from the date appointed for its first meeting, with mandatory elections held before dissolution or within 6 months of dissolution.
importantPoints: Normal 5-year fixed term
Elections must be held before term expiry or within 6 months after supersession
Prevents indefinite bureaucratic rule
memoryTrick: 243E = Elections within 5 years or 6 months post-dissolution.

title: Disqualifications for Membership
explanation: Sets disqualification criteria for panchayat membership, including disqualification under any state law or being less than 21 years of age.
importantPoints: Minimum contesting age is 21 years
State law determines other disqualification parameters
Decided by designated state authority

title: Powers, Authority and Responsibilities of Panchayats
explanation: Empowers state legislatures to endow panchayats with necessary powers to function as institutions of self-government, specifically referencing the 29 matters in the Eleventh Schedule.
importantPoints: Covers 29 functional items in Eleventh Schedule
Includes agriculture, rural development, sanitation, and primary education
Devolution of powers rests with state legislation

title: Powers to Impose Taxes by, and Funds of, the Panchayats
explanation: Permits state legislatures to authorize panchayats to levy, collect, and appropriate taxes, duties, tolls, and fees, and establishes Panchayat Funds.
importantPoints: Financial autonomy provisions
State tax revenue assignments and grants-in-aid
Creation of dedicated Panchayat Funds

title: Constitution of Finance Commission to Review Financial Position
explanation: Mandates the Governor of a state to constitute a Finance Commission every 5 years to review the financial position of panchayats and recommend tax devotions.
importantPoints: Constituted by Governor every 5 years
Recommends tax sharing between state and local bodies
Mirror of national Finance Commission (Article 280)

title: Audit of Accounts of Panchayats
explanation: Empowers state legislatures to make provisions for the maintenance and auditing of panchayat accounts.
importantPoints: Ensures financial accountability
State audit mechanisms applied to grassroots funds

title: Elections to the Panchayats
explanation: Vests superintendence, direction, and control of panchayat electoral rolls and elections in a State Election Commission headed by a State Election Commissioner.
importantPoints: Independent State Election Commission
Commissioner removed like a High Court Judge
Separates local election machinery from Election Commission of India
memoryTrick: 243K = Key election commission for local bodies.

title: Application to Union Territories
explanation: Directs that the provisions of Part IX shall apply to Union Territories, with the President empowered to direct modifications if necessary.
importantPoints: Extends panchayat system to UTs
Presidential notification required for modifications
